summary: Delicious matrix capture of a May ’77 show with monster Sugaree and a smoking High Time, overall superb sound despite a slightly bass‑heavy mix.
review: The performance shines with an outstanding Sugaree, a blazing High Time, tight Jack‑A‑Roe and hot Big River, while the second set stays on fire. The matrix recording is praised as crisp and beautiful, though a few listeners note a bass‑heavy tilt that buries Jerry at times. Overall the show is regarded as one of the stronger 1977 outings.

ai_rating.rationale: The majority of reviews are 5‑star, highlighting exceptional playing and a high‑quality matrix; only minor mix complaints keep the rating just below perfect.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Matrix is described as delicious, beautiful and superbly clear; a few reviewers mention it being bass‑heavy and Jerry sometimes quiet, but overall the mix is balanced and enjoyable.

show_quality.setlist_flow: First set is praised for tight song selection and execution; the second set maintains high energy with a particularly exceptional High Time and strong jams, though some feel the pacing slows slightly compared to earlier ’77 shows.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Generally solid lead work but noted as occasionally buried in the bass‑heavy mix.
band_member_comments.Bob: Weir’s rhythm guitar and vocals come through clearly; his presence is highlighted as a strength of this recording.
band_member_comments.Keys: Keith Godchaux’s keyboards are praised as a highlight of the era and well represented in the mix.
